title: Ontology SDK - OSDK references - Generate OSDK for other languages - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: Ontology SDK that provides generated client libraries for interacting with the Palantir Ontology, with built-in support for Java, Python, and TypeScript

extractedEntities.name: Developer Console
extractedEntities.description: Palantir application for managing application APIs, SDK generation, and exporting API specifications including OpenAPI format

extractedEntities.name: OpenAPI Export
extractedEntities.description: Industry-standard OpenAPI format export capability from Developer Console, enabling code generation for clients in virtually any programming language

extractedEntities.name: SDK Code Generation
extractedEntities.description: Process of generating typed client libraries from the Ontology definition, supporting Java, Python, TypeScript natively and any language via OpenAPI

extractedEntities.name: Multi-Language SDK Generation
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of supporting multiple programming languages by exporting an OpenAPI spec and using open-source generators to create clients beyond the built-in languages

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology concept representing a real-world entity type, exposed through generated OSDK clients

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept for server-side logic, exposed through generated OSDK clients

keyInsights: Developer Console supports exporting APIs in OpenAPI format, enabling OSDK client generation for any language beyond the built-in Java, Python, and TypeScript
The exported OpenAPI file includes resource names and descriptions, so users must ensure these do not contain sensitive information before export
Open-source OpenAPI generators can be used to create both client and server code from the exported specification

title: Functions - TypeScript v1 - Decorators - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Server-side TypeScript logic in Foundry, declared as methods of a class with specific decorators, published to the function registry

extractedEntities.name: @Function Decorator
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript decorator imported from @foundry/functions-api used to mark generic functions for discovery and publication

extractedEntities.name: @OntologyEditFunction Decorator
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript decorator for functions that back an Action, enabling ontology edit operations on Object Types

extractedEntities.name: @Query Decorator
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript decorator for read-only queries callable through Foundry API, requires an apiName parameter, mutually exclusive with @Function

extractedEntities.name: @Edits Decorator
extractedEntities.description: Optional TypeScript decorator specifying object provenance information for @OntologyEditFunction, takes an Object Type parameter

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept representing a mutation operation, backed by functions decorated with @OntologyEditFunction

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity type that can be edited by OntologyEditFunctions and queried by Query functions

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data operating system platform where TypeScript functions are published and executed

extractedEntities.name: @foundry/functions-api
extractedEntities.description: NPM package providing TypeScript decorators and types for declaring Foundry functions

extractedEntities.name: @foundry/ontology-api
extractedEntities.description: NPM package providing typed access to Ontology Object Types within TypeScript functions

extractedEntities.name: Function Registry Publication
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where decorated public methods exported from index.ts are automatically discovered and published to Foundry's function registry

keyInsights: TypeScript v1 functions require three conditions: public method, class exported from index.ts, and decorated with one of @Function, @OntologyEditFunction, or @Query
@OntologyEditFunction backs Actions and can optionally use @Edits to specify object provenance; without @Edits, provenance is inferred via static analysis
@Query is mutually exclusive with @Function and designed specifically for read-only queries exposed through the Foundry API
Each function is uniquely identified by its class name and method name combination; renaming either creates a new function identity
Private or undecorated methods are not published, allowing helper functions and utilities within the same class

title: GitHub - palantir/workshop-iframe-custom-widget: Create custom widgets from applications

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application builder platform that supports custom widgets, variable binding, and event execution

extractedEntities.name: Workshop Iframe Custom Widget
extractedEntities.description: Open-source npm package (@osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget) enabling bidirectional communication between iframed React apps and Workshop parent

extractedEntities.name: useWorkshopContext Hook
extractedEntities.description: React hook that transforms a config definition into a context object, enabling reading/writing Workshop variables and executing events from custom widgets

extractedEntities.name: Iframe Custom Widget Pattern
extractedEntities.description: Architecture pattern for creating custom Workshop widgets by embedding React applications in iframes with bidirectional variable/event communication

extractedEntities.name: ObjectSet
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept representing a collection of objects, supported as a Workshop variable type with a 10,000 object limit for iframe communication

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Concrete entity type required when specifying ObjectSet variables in iframe custom widgets

extractedEntities.name: IConfigDefinition
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript type for defining widget configuration fields including input/output variables, events, and nested listOf fields with type-safe const assertion

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: Ontology SDK that the iframe custom widget integrates with for loading ObjectSets

keyInsights: Workshop iframe custom widgets enable bidirectional communication: Workshop passes variable values to the iframe, and the iframe can set variables and execute events in Workshop
ObjectSet variables require specifying a concrete Object Type and have a 10,000 primary key/objectRid limit for data passed between Workshop and the iframe
Config definitions must use 'as const satisfies IConfigDefinition' for TypeScript to properly infer the context object type from field IDs
This is the first supported path for creating custom Workshop widgets from external applications, representing a significant extensibility milestone
Authentication is handled by the iframe independently — users may see a login screen on first load

title: The Palantir Developer Reformation - Medium

extractedEntities.name: Forward Deployed Engineering Model
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's product development model where engineers embed with customers to solve challenging problems, tightly coupling FDE and PD teams to shape products to match real-world problems

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform with 'Lego block' components used by FDEs to rapidly assemble solutions, historically lacking developer-facing tooling like CLIs

extractedEntities.name: Developer Experience Gap
extractedEntities.description: Structural gap in Palantir's platform caused by the FDE model excluding external developers, resulting in missing CLI tooling, documentation issues, and PD teams not empathizing with developer needs

extractedEntities.name: CLI Absence
extractedEntities.description: Foundry historically lacked a command-line interface because the FDE model did not require one — CLIs are solutions in search of a problem under this lens

extractedEntities.name: ClickOps vs DevOps
extractedEntities.description: Anti-pattern where developers must navigate console UIs manually instead of automating tasks via CLI, contrasting with hyperscaler best practices

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Public interfaces that many parts of Foundry lack, preventing exposure via CLI or external tooling

keyInsights: The FDE model tightly couples Forward Deployed Engineers with Product Development teams, ensuring products match real-world problem shapes rather than theoretical requirements documents
The FDE model historically excluded external developers, creating a developer experience that leaves engineers 'feeling ill upon first exposure' to the platform
Foundry lacked a CLI because the user category that would use one did not exist in sufficient quantity under the FDE model, and many platform parts lack public interfaces
PD teams culturally reframe developer problems as Foundry-shaped solutions rather than building standard developer tooling like CLIs
The recommended 'medicine' for developers is to think more like FDEs and less like software engineers, though this underutilizes engineering capabilities

title: How we evolve APIs - Palantir Blog

extractedEntities.name: Conjure
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's language-independent API declaration framework that generates idiomatic typed bindings for servers and clients, using a restricted set of data types to avoid unintentional API breaks

extractedEntities.name: API Evolution
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's strategy for managing the tension between API stability and agility, preferring backwards-compatible changes and using tooling to guide developers into a pit of success

extractedEntities.name: Backwards-Compatible Evolution
extractedEntities.description: Preferred API change strategy where clients don't notice changes, avoiding breaking changes through careful schema evolution

extractedEntities.name: Conjure API Declaration
extractedEntities.description: YAML-based API specification with typed request/response objects, supporting primitives, collections, and recursive objects but not inheritance

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform whose APIs are declared in Conjure and evolved using backwards-compatible strategies

extractedEntities.name: Gotham
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's government analytics platform whose APIs are also declared in Conjure

extractedEntities.name: Asynchronous Client-Server Updates
extractedEntities.description: Deployment model where clients and servers can be updated independently without coordination, essential for microservice architectures

extractedEntities.name: Conjure Code Generators
extractedEntities.description: Tools that produce idiomatic, language-specific bindings for servers and clients from Conjure API declarations

keyInsights: All Palantir APIs (Foundry and Gotham) are declared in Conjure, a language-independent typed API specification framework that generates idiomatic client/server bindings
Conjure intentionally uses a restricted type set (primitives, collections, recursive objects) and no inheritance, preventing a large class of unintentional API breaks
Palantir's API evolution philosophy: build tooling that naturally leads developers into a 'pit of success' so they focus on design rather than schema change subtleties
The deployment model allows asynchronous, uncoordinated updates of clients and servers — critical for microservice architectures with independent team ownership
Backwards-compatible API evolution is strongly preferred, where clients don't even notice changes have occurred

title: Functions - TypeScript v2 - Getting started - Palantir

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Server-side logic in Foundry, in TypeScript v2 authored as default exports from .ts files with Node.js runtime and first-class OSDK support

extractedEntities.name: TypeScript v2 Functions
extractedEntities.description: Next-generation function authoring in Foundry with Node.js runtime, first-class OSDK support, simplified export patterns (default export), and file-path-based unique identification

extractedEntities.name: TypeScript v1 Functions
extractedEntities.description: Original function authoring approach using class-based decorators, superseded by TypeScript v2 with improved developer experience

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir platform hosting TypeScript v2 functions with live preview, commit-based publishing, and tag-based versioning

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: Ontology SDK with first-class support in TypeScript v2 functions, enabling typed Ontology interaction

extractedEntities.name: Function Registry
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's centralized registry where published functions are discoverable and executable from Workshop and other applications

extractedEntities.name: Live Preview
extractedEntities.description: Development feature allowing real-time function testing via the Functions helper before committing

extractedEntities.name: File-Path Function Identity
extractedEntities.description: TypeScript v2 pattern where a function's file path uniquely identifies the published function; changing the path creates a new function

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir application builder where published functions can be searched and used

keyInsights: TypeScript v2 functions use default exports from named .ts files instead of decorated class methods, with the filename matching the function name
TypeScript v2 brings a Node.js runtime and first-class OSDK support as key improvements over v1's decorator-based approach
Functions are published through a commit-then-tag workflow: commit to master, then tag a version to publish all functions in the repository
A function's file path serves as its unique identifier — changing the path results in a new function being published rather than updating the existing one
Functions may not be immediately searchable by name in Workshop while permissions propagate after publishing

title: AIP Virtual Tables

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that integrates generative AI with organizational data systems, algorithms, and decision-making workflows

extractedEntities.name: Virtual Table
extractedEntities.description: Connectivity solution that uses external systems as backing sources of truth for Ontology data elements without data replication or duplication

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform whose data layer can be used alongside or instead of Virtual Tables for data processing

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: The semantic data model that Virtual Tables can populate by registering external data sources without redundant copies

extractedEntities.name: Virtual Connectivity
extractedEntities.description: Architecture pattern connecting data assets from external data lakes, lakehouses, and platforms into AIP without data duplication

extractedEntities.name: Predicate Pushdown
extractedEntities.description: Performance optimization in Virtual Tables that harnesses native features of connected data platforms for better query performance

extractedEntities.name: Material Shortages Management
extractedEntities.description: Supply chain use case where AIP identifies, prioritizes, and responds to material shortages using LLMs to reason through previous notes and actions, achieving 25% productivity increase

extractedEntities.name: Nurse Scheduling
extractedEntities.description: Healthcare use case where AIP generates optimized shift schedules considering preferences, costs, and demand predictions

extractedEntities.name: Train Routing
extractedEntities.description: Rail company use case where AIP surfaces areas at risk for high railcar dwell and builds remediation plans through natural language interaction

keyInsights: Virtual Tables enable using external data platforms as backing sources of truth for the Ontology without data replication, providing a fast track to getting started with AIP
Organizations maintain architectural flexibility — they can choose when to use data in-place vs Palantir's data layer, and change this over time without impacting AIP workflows
Virtual Tables support predicate pushdown to connected platforms, improving performance and reducing costs by minimizing data storage and transfer
Virtual Tables were announced at AIPCon as a general availability feature, enabling users to begin building AIP workflows in minutes from AIP Bootcamp onward
The feature preserves architectural optionality, allowing teams to evolve their data strategy without being locked into a single approach

title: Beyond Anonymization (Palantir Explained, #3)

extractedEntities.name: De-identification
extractedEntities.description: Process of stripping data of direct identifiers like names and addresses while maintaining legal protections, preferred over the term 'anonymization' which conveys a false sense of security

extractedEntities.name: Re-identification Risk
extractedEntities.description: Risk of tracing de-identified or anonymized data back to the individual it describes, addressed at both data level and usage circumstance level

extractedEntities.name: Purpose-Based Access Controls
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's access control mechanism referenced as part of their broader data protection framework

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that implements frameworks and technology to help customers reduce re-identification risks

extractedEntities.name: Two-Level Re-identification Mitigation
extractedEntities.description: Approach to reducing re-identification risk at two levels: the data itself (sensitivity, identifiability, joinability) and the circumstances of its use

extractedEntities.name: GDPR Compliance
extractedEntities.description: European data protection regulation that governs anonymization requirements and data subject rights

keyInsights: Palantir recommends using 'de-identification' instead of 'anonymization' because anonymization conveys a false sense of security by implying data cannot be re-identified
Re-identification risk must be addressed at two levels: the data itself (sensitivity, identifiability, joinability with other data) and the circumstances in which it is used
De-identified data retains the same legal protections as identifiable data, acknowledging the real risk of re-identification
Palantir has implemented frameworks for customers handling highly sensitive data, such as the NIH's National COVID Cohort Collaborative (N3C)

title: Building with Palantir AIP: AI-Powered Process Mining

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform used for deploying AI-centric process mining and automation workflows that save time, money, and resources

extractedEntities.name: HyperAuto
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's Software-Defined Data Integration (SDDI) technology that connects to ERP/CRM systems like SAP, Salesforce, Oracle NetSuite, and Hubspot, programmatically generating data pipelines

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Decision-centric model of the enterprise where process understanding is incorporated and AI-powered solutions are deployed

extractedEntities.name: AI-Powered Process Mining
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of using AIP to understand operational process reality, identify bottlenecks, and deploy AI-driven copilots to address them

extractedEntities.name: Credit Block Resolution
extractedEntities.description: Order-to-cash process use case where AIP identifies and removes problematic credit blocks that delay order fulfillment for low-risk customers

extractedEntities.name: Software-Defined Data Integration
extractedEntities.description: Technology approach (SDDI) that automates connecting to and extracting data from ERP/CRM systems, programmatically generating pipelines

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir platform providing the data layer and pipeline infrastructure for process mining workflows

keyInsights: AIP process mining follows a two-phase approach: understanding operational reality of how processes actually function, then making better decisions faster using AI-centric logic
HyperAuto (SDDI) connects to common ERP/CRM systems and programmatically generates data pipelines that clean, normalize, and harmonize datasets at speed
Process understanding is incorporated into the Palantir Ontology as the decision-centric model, enabling AI-powered solutions to address bottlenecks
The example demonstrates the full pipeline: sync data with HyperAuto, mine processes with AIP, incorporate into Ontology, deploy AI copilots for credit block resolution

title: Data Cataloging: Bringing order to chaos

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's operating system for the modern enterprise, designed to handle data management pain points at scale including cataloging, governance, and organization

extractedEntities.name: Data Cataloging
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of creating an inventory of all data on a platform — storing, collecting, searching, surfacing, and analyzing information with metadata management and governance context

extractedEntities.name: Data Governance
extractedEntities.description: Framework for enforcing data protection on the platform through transparency, metadata management, and cataloging tools

extractedEntities.name: Metadata Management
extractedEntities.description: Centralized management of data context including profiles, quality concerns, handling requirements, PII tracking, and approval status

extractedEntities.name: PII Tracking
extractedEntities.description: Capability to track which datasets include personally identifiable information, stored alongside the data in the catalog

extractedEntities.name: Data Quality Management
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of capturing and maintaining data quality concerns, data profiles, and handling context within the data catalog

keyInsights: Data catalogs serve five key functions: information capture, data inventory, metadata management, data handling context, and standardized operating procedures
Advancing data protection regulation requires organizations to know not just what data they collect, but where all sensitive customer data resides
As Foundry operating systems scale with more users and data volume, data catalogs become essential for bringing order to complexity
Data catalogs funnel users into a single place to uniformly capture information about data and set up SOPs before data is uploaded or shared

title: Designing for Deletion (Palantir Explained, #6)

extractedEntities.name: Data Deletion Framework
extractedEntities.description: Systematic process for making data less accessible over time, progressing from soft deletion (reversible) to hard deletion (irreversible), with auditing and accountability

extractedEntities.name: Soft Deletion
extractedEntities.description: Reversible stage of deletion where front-end user access is removed but data can still be recovered within a predefined period

extractedEntities.name: Hard Deletion
extractedEntities.description: End goal of deletion process where data is made irreversibly inaccessible and can no longer be processed

extractedEntities.name: GDPR Right to Erasure
extractedEntities.description: GDPR provision affording data subjects the right to request erasure of personal data, also known as the 'right to be forgotten'

extractedEntities.name: Data Minimization
extractedEntities.description: Core data protection principle limiting personal data processing to what is necessary and proportional for legitimate processing purposes

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that helps customers design and implement effective deletion processes as part of their data protection framework

keyInsights: Deletion is defined as a staged process of making data less accessible over time, not a single event — progressing from removing front-end access to soft deletion to eventual hard deletion
What matters most is the result, not the process: when data is deleted, it must no longer be possible to process it
Organizations must balance immediacy of deletion with reversibility — errors happen, so soft deletion periods allow recovery before hard deletion
Deletion frameworks require understanding what data the organization holds, how it is structured, and how it is processed in accordance with regulatory requirements
The 'right to be forgotten' under GDPR specifically grants data subjects the right to request erasure, making deletion capability a legal requirement

title: How Palantir AIP Accelerates Data Migration

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir AI Platform that maintains comprehensive context across the entire migration lifecycle, enabling step-change improvements over traditional point-solution AI approaches

extractedEntities.name: Octopus Model
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's architecture for enterprise data migration where AIP maintains contextual awareness across the entire lifecycle with scoped control for end-to-end workflows

extractedEntities.name: Point-Solution AI Limitation
extractedEntities.description: Anti-pattern where AI is deployed at the team level to automate specific tasks but fails to address the structural problem of separated teams requiring manual reconciliation

extractedEntities.name: ERP Data Migration
extractedEntities.description: Enterprise use case where AIP orchestrates migration from legacy ERP systems (SAP, Oracle) including data extraction, code interpretation, transformation, and validation

extractedEntities.name: Contextual Migration Architecture
extractedEntities.description: AIP's ability to maintain comprehensive context across extraction, interpretation, transformation, consolidation, and validation phases of migration

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir platform providing the underlying infrastructure for AIP-accelerated data migrations

keyInsights: Traditional data migrations fail because separated teams (extraction, transformation, validation) produce separated outputs requiring manual reconciliation, creating endless validation loops
Point-solution AI that accelerates individual tasks delivers only linear improvement — organizations move faster through each phase but remain trapped in the same sequential, error-prone process
AIP's 'Octopus Model' maintains comprehensive context across the entire migration lifecycle with scoped control to implement end-to-end workflows
AIP enables completing complex enterprise migrations in months rather than years by maintaining cross-phase contextual awareness and deploying AI-accelerated workflows

title: How Palantir Foundry Fuels Your Data Platform

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's operating system designed to supercharge existing data platforms through software-defined data integration and decision orchestration

extractedEntities.name: HyperAuto
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's technology encoding Software-Defined Data Integration (SDDI), connecting to ERP/CRM data stores and automating integration into data platforms

extractedEntities.name: Software-Defined Data Integration
extractedEntities.description: Approach that automates integration of ERP/CRM data through intelligent pipeline generation, metadata inference, and data harmonization

extractedEntities.name: Decision Orchestration
extractedEntities.description: Pattern enabling dynamic flow of data, models, and decisions across systems and teams, going beyond the linear 'assembly line' approach to data and analytics

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Generation
extractedEntities.description: SDDI capability that automatically generates complete, efficient data pipelines by reading the shape of data and relationships in source systems

extractedEntities.name: Metadata Inference
extractedEntities.description: SDDI capability that learns from source data shape and adapts dynamic pipeline generation, seamlessly handling business-specific customizations

extractedEntities.name: Data Harmonization
extractedEntities.description: SDDI capability to determine or recommend matches between datasets across different source systems like Oracle NetSuite, SAP S/4HANA, and Salesforce

keyInsights: Foundry supercharges existing data platforms in two ways: software-defined data integration (unlocking operational core) and decision orchestration (closing the feedback loop)
Traditional data platform architectures provide a linear 'assembly line' that summarizes reality but cannot operationalize it — Foundry enables dynamic, self-optimizing flows
HyperAuto (SDDI) automates ERP/CRM integration in hours, with intelligent pipeline generation that adapts to source system shape and business customizations
Data harmonization across different source systems (Oracle NetSuite, SAP S/4HANA, Salesforce) is achieved through automatic entity resolution recommendations

title: How Palantir Foundry's Ontology Deploys Data Science to the Front Line

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's semantic model that collates all organizational entities, relationships, processes, and events in one place, forming a feedback loop between operational actions and the models that informed them

extractedEntities.name: Action
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept representing real-world business processes and decisions, linked to data objects and surfaced as data science targets

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform where the Ontology enables data scientists to connect models with real-world decisions through a feedback loop

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Feedback Loop
extractedEntities.description: Architecture pattern where operational actions feed back into the models that informed them, enabling data scientists to quantify impact through changed behavior

extractedEntities.name: Data Science Target Surfacing
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where the Ontology captures business processes and Actions, linking them to data objects that are surfaced as data science targets

extractedEntities.name: Data Scientist Workflow Optimization
extractedEntities.description: Use case where the Ontology eliminates traditional bottlenecks: slow data discovery, data procurement, and model delivery, enabling reactive-to-proactive shift

keyInsights: The Ontology forms a feedback loop between operational actions and the models that informed them, enabling data scientists to quantify model impact through changed behavior
The Ontology collates all organizational entities, relationships, processes, and events in one place, revealing bottlenecks and opportunities without requiring SME consultations
Actions in the Ontology are linked to data objects that are surfaced as data science targets, eliminating the need to dive into data lakes for dataset discovery
The Ontology flips the data scientist role from reactive (receiving directives) to proactive (viewing all systems and interconnections directly)
Data procurement that traditionally takes months is significantly shortened because the Ontology intuitively helps data scientists find relevant data

title: Inside the AIPCon 8 Demos Redefining the Future of Enterprise AI

keyInsights: Andretti RaceOS processes tens of thousands of data points per second across thousands of sensor channels with 10-1000Hz sampling frequencies, demonstrating the Ontology's ability to handle extreme data volumes
IndyCar telemetry requires synthesizing structured sensor data with unstructured intelligence (weather, tire degradation, driver feedback, historical archives) through the Ontology
Enterprise-specific Ontology is described as the key differentiator that unlocks AI's 'limitless potential' when paired with AIP — across healthcare, motorsport, defense, and retail
AIPCon 8 demonstrated breadth of Palantir's platform across wildfire mitigation, computer vision for retail, secure government AI, and motorsport analytics
